approval.cvss-help: This is the CVSS version 3.x base score, which ranges from 0 to 10 and is calculated from the advisory's CVSS vector. A higher number means a more severe vulnerability. A score of 9.0 or above is critical, 7.0 or above is high, 4.0 or above is medium, and anything above 0 is low.
approval.deny-version-hint: Blocks one exact version even when the package is approved. Applies immediately, including already-cached copies.
approval.deny-version-note: Only this exact version is blocked; other versions keep flowing. Cached copies stop being served immediately.
approval.description: Review quarantined package requests across proxy repositories.
approval.no-approvers: No users currently have approve permission for this repository.
approval.not-scanned: Not scanned yet. The scan runs asynchronously after the request is queued; reload in a moment.
approval.queue-description: Quarantine queue for proxied packages. Approve or reject pending requests before a proxy serves them, and block specific poisoned versions outright.
approval.title: Approvals
approval.version-unknown: unknown (metadata request blocked before a version was resolved)
common.name: Name
common.name-rule: Letters, digits, dash and underscore only.
ha.description: Review storage topology, leader election state, and fencing token for the active cluster.
ha.failover-note: Manual failover gracefully steps this leader down so a standby is elected and takes over. The leader releases its lease and traffic moves to the new leader. This pod stays Ready and can be re-elected later. Use it for planned maintenance or to drain this pod. Expect a brief in-flight disruption while traffic moves.
ha.step-down-confirm-message: This leader gracefully releases its lease and a standby is elected. Traffic moves to the new leader. Expect a brief disruption. This pod stays Ready and can be re-elected later.
ha.stepping-down-notice: Stepping down — a standby is taking over. Traffic follows the new leader.
ha.title: HA Status
login.access-note: Access is limited by your assigned role.
login.description: Manage repository policies, approvals, and scoped tokens.
login.title: Sign in to forklift
notification.delete-confirm: Repositories selecting this receiver will stop notifying it. This cannot be undone.
notification.description: A receiver is a webhook (Slack/Mattermost-compatible) alerted when a package enters a repository's approval queue. Each repository chooses which receivers to notify in its Settings.
notification.empty: No receivers yet. Add one to start sending approval alarms.
notification.subtitle: · alarm channels
notification.title: Notifications
notification.webhook-note: The webhook URL is never shown again after it is saved. Leave it blank when editing to keep the current URL.
notification.webhook-warning: The webhook URL cannot be viewed again after you save it. You can only replace it with a new one. Keep a copy somewhere safe before you save.
repo.allow-licenses: Allow licenses (SPDX id, one per line; empty = allow all but denied)
repo.approval-audit: Package approval is in audit mode. Requests are logged but packages are still served.
repo.approval-on: Package approval is on. An admin must approve a package before this proxy serves it.
repo.approvers-subtitle: · users who can approve this repository
repo.basics-description: Choose the protocol and serving mode. The generated endpoint follows this selection.
repo.block-unresolved: Block versions not yet resolved (block action only)
repo.block-unscanned: Block versions not yet scanned (block action only)
repo.cooldown-label: Block versions newer than a cooldown window
repo.cooldown-note: Use this when newly published packages should cool down before serving.
repo.delete-confirm: This removes the repository and its cached/hosted artifacts. This cannot be undone.
repo.delete-note: Deleting a repository removes its cached/hosted artifacts. This cannot be undone.
repo.deny-licenses: Deny licenses (SPDX id, one per line)
repo.license-gate: Gate packages by their declared license
repo.license-note: Licenses are resolved per version from deps.dev. A denied license, or any license outside a non-empty allow list, triggers the selected action. Matching is case-insensitive.
repo.list-description: Host and proxy artifacts across Maven, npm, Cargo, Go, and PyPI. Configure per-repository caching and supply-chain policies (age cooldown, package approval).
repo.members-note: Select member repositories in lookup order. The first hit wins.
repo.new-description: Register a hosted, proxy, or group repository for package delivery.
repo.no-access-role: No role grants access to this repository. Add a permission on a role (Roles page) whose pattern matches this repository.
repo.no-access-token: No access token reaches this repository.
repo.no-cached-artifacts: No cached artifacts yet. Pull through the endpoint above to populate the cache.
repo.offline-note: This repository is offline: uploads and downloads are refused (503). Its config and artifacts are kept.
repo.online-note: This repository is online and serving the package protocols.
repo.policy-order-desc: A proxy request flows through each gate in this order — the source-IP ACL first, then the supply-chain policies. Dimmed gates are disabled. A
repo.policy-order-desc-2: and audit gates only log and let it continue.
repo.proxy-description: Configure the remote registry and optional supply-chain cooldown.
repo.purge-confirm: This removes every cached or hosted artifact in the repository. The repository and its settings stay. A proxy will re-cache on demand; hosted uploads cannot be recovered.
repo.purge-note: Purging removes all cached/hosted artifacts but keeps the repository and its settings. This cannot be undone.
repo.quarantine-desc: · alarm a receiver when a package is quarantined
repo.readonly-note: Read-only view. You can review the configuration but not change it.
repo.receivers-note: Selected receivers get a webhook alarm when a package here enters the approval queue. Manage receivers on the Notifications page.
repo.require-approval: Require approval before serving a package
repo.restrict-by-ip: Restrict access by client source IP
repo.type.group-desc: Combine repositories behind a single read-only URL
repo.type.hosted-desc: Store artifacts uploaded directly by your team
repo.type.proxy-desc: Cache and serve artifacts from an upstream registry
role.delete-confirm: Users and group mappings holding this role lose its permissions immediately. This cannot be undone.
role.empty: No roles yet. Create one to grant repository access.
role.list-description: Bundle repository permissions (read, write, delete, approve, audit, admin) over name patterns. Open a role to map permissions; roles are assigned to users on each user's detail page.
role.managed-note: This role was configured by a Forklift administrator in the declarative RBAC policy so it cannot be edited here. To change its permissions or delete it ask an administrator to update the policy file and restart forklift.
role.new-description: Define a reusable access profile and optional repository permissions.
role.no-users: No users have this role. Assign it from a user's detail page.
role.permissions-description: Add repository patterns and allowed actions. Wildcards are accepted.
settings.appearance-description: Choose a fixed theme or follow your operating system setting.
settings.card-description: These settings apply only to the current browser.
settings.description: Preferences are saved in this browser until account-level settings are available.
settings.language-description: Store the preferred interface language for this browser.
settings.language.en-description: Use English as the preferred UI language.
settings.language.ko-description: Use Korean as the preferred UI language.
settings.title: Settings
token.copy-warning: Copy this token now. It will not be shown again.
token.description-placeholder: What this token is used for
token.expiry-note: Tokens expire after at most one year.
token.help-1: Scoped credentials for package clients, limited to chosen repositories and actions within your own permissions. Use a token as the password in your package manager (npm
token.incomplete-note: Complete all required fields before creating a token.
token.name: Token name
token.new-description: Issue a scoped access token with an expiration date and repository permissions.
token.permissions-description: Add repository patterns and allowed token actions.
token.readonly-note: Read-only: only administrators can create or revoke tokens.
token.revoke-confirm-message: Clients using this token will immediately lose access. This cannot be undone.
token.subtitle: · scoped credentials for package clients
token.title: Access tokens
user.delete-note: Deleting a user revokes all of their tokens and role assignments. This cannot be undone.
user.delete-self-note: You cannot delete your own account.
user.initial-password-note: Set an initial password for this local account.
user.list-description: Local and OIDC accounts. Open a user to map roles, reset the password, or disable access. OIDC users appear automatically at first login.
user.locked-note: Locked after too many failed password attempts — unlock it in Account lockout.
user.lockout-note: When enabled, the account is locked after 5 consecutive failed password attempts and must be unlocked by an administrator.
user.lockout-protected-note: The default admin account cannot be locked out.
user.new-description: Create a local account and optionally assign an initial role.
user.no-role-note: A user with no role cannot access repositories until one is assigned.
user.oidc-note: OIDC users are created automatically at first login.
user.protected-note: This is the default administrator account, created when Forklift first started. Its status is locked active so the system always keeps at least one administrator who can sign in and recover access. It cannot be disabled by anyone, including other administrators.
user.self-note: You are signed in as this account, so you cannot disable it yourself. This guards against accidentally locking yourself out of Forklift. If this account needs to be disabled, ask another administrator to do it.
user.status-desc-1: account can sign in to Forklift and use its credentials to pull and publish artifacts, while
user.status-desc-2: immediately revokes that access by stopping all sessions and tokens. The account, its tokens and its role assignments are preserved, so disabling is a reversible alternative to deletion that you can undo at any time by re-enabling.
